title: Incident Reporting to Authorities — Regulatory Timer Watch

compliance.assurance_rationale: NIST 800-53 IR-6 — Reviewed: SPL provides contributing evidence for this regulatory requirement. The search query directly addresses the monitoring objective specified by the clause.

spl:
`notable` earliest=-30d tag="regulated_breach"
| eval hours_open=round((now()-_time)/3600,1)
| where hours_open>72 AND status!="Closed"
| table _time, rule_name, owner, status, hours_open
description: Adds timer fields for regulatory reporting obligations tied to incidents for IR-6 incident reporting.
value: Gives the responsible team auditor-ready evidence for NIST 800-53 compliance by automating regulatory compliance evidence. Replaces manual evidence gathering with a continuous Splunk search that produces structured, time-stamped artefacts suitable for regulatory examination and internal audit review.
implementation: (1) Standardize tags for regulated incidents; (2) integrate legal workflow fields; (3) alert before deadlines; (4) document reporting chains per jurisdiction.
visualization: Timeline, Table (aging regulated incidents), Single value (past threshold). Key fields: `hours_open`, `owner`, `rule_name`, `status`.
knownFalsePositives: Incident response time variance during shift handoffs, after-hours, or major incident bursts.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Enterprise Security (Splunkbase 263).
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `` `notable` `` with `category` or `tag` for regulated incidents.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Standardize tags for regulated incidents; (2) integrate legal workflow fields; (3) alert before deadlines; (4) document reporting chains per jurisdiction.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
`notable` earliest=-30d tag="regulated_breach"
| eval hours_open=round((now()-_time)/3600,1)
| where hours_open>72 AND status!="Closed"
| table _time, rule_name, owner, status, hours_open
```

Understanding this SPL

**Incident Reporting to Authorities — Regulatory Timer Watch (IR-6)** — Adds timer fields for regulatory reporting obligations tied to incidents for IR-6 incident reporting.

Documented **Data sources**: `` `notable` `` with `category` or `tag` for regulated incidents. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Enterprise Security (Splunkbase 263). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Invokes macro `notable` — in Search, use the UI or expand to inspect the underlying SPL.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **hours_open** — applying business logic to classify, threshold, or derive compliance-relevant indicators.
• Filters the current rows with `where hours_open>72 AND status!="Closed"` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.

Output fields: _time, hours_open, owner, rule_name, status.

Step 3 — Validate
Compare a sample of the same events in the source system (domain controller, cloud tenant, or device) the security team would open first. Spot-check a few source events, reconcile counts, and verify field extractions, thresholds, and index permissions for the people who need this evidence.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Timeline, Table (aging regulated incidents), Single value (past threshold)
grandmaExplanation: We adds timer fields for regulatory reporting obligations tied to incidents for IR-6 incident reporting. That helps federal and agency security reviews match what the systems actually do day to day.
